topic: I replaced my $80k VA with AI agents
hook_summary: I fired my VA. Now I run 3 businesses with AI agents for $400/mo.

scenes.audio_text: I fired my $80,000-a-year virtual assistant last quarter. And my businesses run better without her.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up of @daniel in a bright, modern home office with natural light from a window behind. He sits at a wooden desk, leaning forward slightly. Camera locked-off, candid documentary style, slight lens flare on the right. He looks directly into the lens, confident expression.

scenes.audio_text: I built three AI agents—one for inbound email triage, one for scheduling, and one for customer support triage. Cost me $400 a month total.
scenes.visual_prompt: Over-the-shoulder shot of @daniel at his desk, typing on a laptop. Screen shows a simple dashboard with three agent status indicators. Warm, natural lighting from the window. Slow push-in on his face as he speaks. Photo-real, casual.

scenes.audio_text: Response time dropped from 4 hours to 90 seconds. Customer satisfaction score went up 12 points. I wasn't losing quality—I was losing overhead.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium shot of @daniel walking down a city street, coffee in hand, talking to the camera. Golden hour light, soft shadows. He gestures with his free hand. Documentary style, locked-off camera.

scenes.audio_text: Everyone told me you need a human touch. They were wrong. You need the right system. That's it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Medium close-up of @daniel leaning against a brick wall, arms crossed, slight smirk. Evening light, urban background with blurred passersby. Camera locked-off, shallow depth of field. Natural, candid.

loop_back_line: I fired my $80,000-a-year VA. Now my businesses run better without her.
